brief: Crude oil prices have risen above $70 per barrel due to escalating US-Iran tensions, which may impact the global economy and inflation. Meanwhile, in India, the government is considering a subsidy to help MSME exporters mitigate the effects of the EU's Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism, and the RBI has opened the term money market to All India Financial Institutions and housing finance companies to deepen market participation. Additionally, India's startup ecosystem has seen a strong funding comeback, with venture funding crossing the $1 billion mark in a week, driven largely by significant investments in technology startups.

Geopolitics.title: Crude oil prices rise amid escalating US-Iran war, supply fears; Brent above $70 per barrel
Geopolitics.summary: The escalating US-Iran war has led to a rise in crude oil prices, with Brent crude gaining 0.36% to trade at $72.25 a barrel and US benchmark West Texas Intermediate crude futures rising 0.71% to $69.72 a barrel. This increase is due to supply fears, and it may have implications for the global economy. The rise in oil prices can impact inflation, trade, and economic growth.

Geopolitics.title: The Nord Stream Conspiracy: An international war story in blockbuster prose
Geopolitics.summary: The book 'The Nord Stream Conspiracy' by Bojan Pancevski investigates the planning, execution, and fallout of the 2022 pipeline sabotage. The sabotage has significant implications for international relations, energy security, and global politics. The book provides an in-depth look at the events surrounding the sabotage and its impact on the global landscape.

Geopolitics.title: Centre Eyes Subsidy To Ease EU Carbon Border Tax Burden On MSME Exporters: Report
Geopolitics.summary: The Indian government is considering a subsidy to help MSME exporters mitigate the impact of the EU's Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM). The CBAM is a tax on imports from countries with less stringent climate policies, and it may disproportionately affect Indian exporters, particularly in the iron and steel sector. The subsidy aims to ease the burden on MSME exporters and help them remain competitive in the global market.

Economy.title: NRI money could ease banks' funding crunch before powering India's capex: Axis Bank CEO
Economy.summary: Axis Bank CEO Amitabh Chaudhry says Indian banks will leverage new foreign-currency deposits from NRIs to replace costly funding, which will be used to support infrastructure, data centers, and real estate. This move is expected to ease pressure from a widening gap between loan and deposit growth. The influx of funds will also help support the rupee.

Economy.title: Weekly economy wrap: Core sector slowdown, US trade talks shape policy week
Economy.summary: India's policy week was marked by US trade talks, a slowdown in core sector output, and stronger foreign direct investment. The government also pushed for coal-import substitution and faster disinvestment. These developments are expected to shape the country's economic policy.

Economy.title: Explained: How India’s transition from WPI to PPI will strengthen inflation measurement - what it means for policy making
Economy.summary: India is transitioning from the Wholesale Price Index (WPI) to the Producer Price Index (PPI) to measure inflation. This change is expected to strengthen inflation measurement and improve policy making. The new index will provide a more accurate picture of price changes in the economy.

Economy.title: RBI opens term money market to AIFIs, housing finance companies
Economy.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has opened the term money market to All India Financial Institutions and housing finance companies, allowing them to borrow and lend. This move aims to deepen market participation, improve liquidity, and strengthen monetary policy transmission by linking short-term and long-term interest rates.

Business.title: IIFCL plans $1 billion overseas loan, also eyes $400 million ADB funding to back infrastructure push
Business.summary: India Infrastructure Finance Company Ltd (IIFCL) is planning to raise $1 billion from overseas investors to support the country's infrastructure development. This move is part of the government's efforts to boost infrastructure growth. The funding will help IIFCL to finance various infrastructure projects in India.

Business.title: Where are the unicorns of 2021
Business.summary: In 2021, 39 Indian startups achieved unicorn status, but now they are navigating a changed landscape with global uncertainties and investor caution. Some of these startups are securing significant investments, while others are adapting their strategies or pursuing public listings. This reflects the evolving dynamics of the startup ecosystem in India.

Business.title: Why Equirus is avoiding loss-making startups in its ₹1,500 crore fund
Business.summary: Equirus is launching a new fund of ₹1,500 crore, but it will avoid investing in loss-making startups. This approach reflects the changing startup funding market in India, which has become more selective. The fund will focus on investing in startups with strong growth potential and financial discipline.

Business.title: India startup funding tops $1 billion as CRED mega
Business.summary: India's startup ecosystem has seen a strong funding comeback, with venture funding crossing the $1 billion mark in a week. This was driven largely by CRED's significant fundraising round. The surge in funding reflects the growing interest of investors in the Indian startup space.

Business.title: India's startup pipeline narrows despite rise in unicorns and IPOs in H1
Business.summary: Despite a rise in unicorns and IPOs, India's startup pipeline has narrowed in the first half of 2026. Technology startups raised $7.2 billion across 652 equity funding rounds during this period. This trend reflects the evolving nature of the startup ecosystem in India, with a focus on consolidation and growth.

Business.title: India tech funding rises 12 pc to $7.2 billion in H1 2026: Report
Business.summary: India's technology startup ecosystem attracted $7.2 billion in funding during the first half of 2026, a 12% increase from the previous year. This growth reflects the strong demand for technology solutions in India and the growing interest of investors in the sector. The funding will help startups to scale up their operations and expand their services.

National Affairs.title: BJP seeks cancellation of Kannur Corporation councillors' oath over 'Allah' reference
National Affairs.summary: The BJP has challenged the oaths of 19 councillors in Kannur Corporation, alleging they violated statutory forms by referencing 'Allah' during their oath-taking ceremony. This controversy highlights the complexities of oath-taking in a diverse country like India. The Kerala High Court's ruling will be crucial in resolving this issue.

National Affairs.title: 'Heavens are not going to fall': SC refuses urgent hearing on plea seeking CBI probe into Ayodhya Ram Mandir donation theft row
National Affairs.summary: The Supreme Court has refused an urgent hearing on a petition seeking a CBI probe into the alleged theft of donations at the Ayodhya Ram Mandir. The court's decision indicates that it does not consider the matter urgent, and a regular hearing will be scheduled. This case has sparked controversy and demands for a thorough investigation.

National Affairs.title: Law must act as shield for innocent, not as a sword for the vindictive: Supreme Court
National Affairs.summary: The Supreme Court has emphasized that the law should protect the innocent and not be used to harass or victimize individuals. The court highlighted the social stigma and harm caused by criminal proceedings, stressing the need for careful consideration in such cases. This statement underscores the importance of upholding justice and protecting individual rights.

National Affairs.title: People Have A Fundamental Right To Clean Water
National Affairs.summary: The Bombay High Court has reaffirmed that access to clean water is a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Constitution. The court criticized the Maharashtra government for failing to ensure adequate water supply, citing the public trust doctrine and the need for stronger implementation to address rising pollution and water stress. This ruling emphasizes the importance of access to clean water as a basic human right.

National Affairs.title: Supreme Court Backs Homebuyers Rights, Says Compensation Can Be Claimed Even After Taking Flat Possession
National Affairs.summary: The Supreme Court has ruled that homebuyers can claim compensation for delayed delivery even after taking possession of their flats. This verdict is a significant relief for homebuyers and sets an important precedent for similar disputes across India. The court's decision aims to protect the rights of homebuyers and promote fairness in the real estate sector.
